title: UNIX File and Directory System Hierarchy
description: A visual representation of the hierarchical structure of the UNIX filesystem, showing the relationship between directories and files, and key system calls for manipulation.
relationships: Hierarchical tree structure starting from root
System calls like open(), read(), write(), close() manipulate file descriptors
File descriptors index into kernel-managed tables that point to inodes
Directories are special files containing mappings of filenames to inode numbers
